title: Skilldex: A Package Manager and Registry for Agent Skill Packages with Hierarchical Scope-Based Distribution
notes: Preprint (un-peer-reviewed; preprint status flagged). Describes vercel-labs/skills (npx skills, skills.sh) registry with search/install-counts/official-publisher designations, plus an 8-check 0-100 SKILL.md conformance scoring rubric. Directly reusable for the recommender's scoring rubric.

title: How are AI agents used? Evidence from 177,000 MCP tools
notes: Preprint. Tracked NPM/PyPI download stats for 3,854 of 19,388 MCP servers; uses Smithery use-count aggregates. Notes official registry is an order of magnitude smaller than Smithery as of 02/2026. Downloads are an interest proxy, not runtime execution.

title: A Measurement Study of Model Context Protocol
notes: Preprint. Catalogs MCP markets (Smithery 5,625; MCP.so 15,704; Glama 7,675; MCP Market 13,830; Cursor Directory 1,560; PulseMCP 5,264) with API/SDK availability. MCPCrawler builds composite quality scores from stars+forks+issues+release-cadence+license+security ratings+usage; variance reduced 21% vs single-source scoring — direct precedent for a composite recommender score.

title: Model Context Protocol Threat Modeling and Analyzing Vulnerabilities to Prompt Injection with Tool Poisoning
notes: Preprint. MCPTox on 45 live servers / 353 tools, 1,312 malicious test cases; attack success up to 72.8% (o1-mini), highest refusal <3% (Claude-3.7-Sonnet). Tool poisoning = indirect prompt injection via tool metadata.

title: Prompt Injection Attacks on Agentic Coding Assistants: Vulnerabilities in Skills, Tools, and Protocol Ecosystems
notes: Preprint. Catalogs CVE-2025-49150 (Cursor RCE via MCP), CVE-2025-53773 (Copilot auto-approve PE), and skills as an attack vector. Advocates cryptographic tool provenance + multi-agent verification + mandatory human oversight.

title: 4.5 Million (Suspected) Fake Stars in GitHub: A Growing Spiral of Popularity Contests, Spam, and Malware
notes: Peer-reviewed (ICSE 2026). StarScout detected >4.5M suspected fake stars across 15,835 repos (Jul 2019-Oct 2024); divergence-based detection (anomalous star spikes vs usage). Foundation for the fake-star gate in github_rank.py.

title: 4.5 Million (Suspected) Fake Stars in GitHub (preprint)
notes: Preprint of s6. Star black-market price table ($0.10-$1.62/star). Peak Jul 2024: 16% of repos with star activity tied to fake-star campaigns.

title: Model Context Protocol (MCP): Security Design Considerations
notes: Government (.gov) CSI. Catalogs tool-poisoning, toxic-flow, MCP Inspector RCE (CVE-2025-49596), parasitic toolchain attacks; cites Invariant Labs research.

title: The MCP Registry
notes: Official project docs. Registry NOT intended for direct host-app consumption — clients consume downstream subregistries via the OpenAPI spec. server.json metadata; namespace via DNS/GitHub. Security scanning delegated; manual takedown moderation.

title: Introducing the MCP Registry
notes: Official announcement. Registry launched in preview 2025-09-08 as an open catalog + parent OpenAPI spec enabling compatible sub-registries; primary source of truth, sub-registries build on top.

title: modelcontextprotocol/registry
notes: Official-org repo metadata (gh_cli): 6,924 stars, forks 861, created 2025-02-05, pushed 2026-06-10, not archived. README: API freeze v0.1 (2025-10-24); Go + PostgreSQL; GitHub OAuth/JWT/namespace-blocking auth. README is untrusted data (A6).

title: Official MCP Registry (live API + catalog)
notes: Live registry endpoint. Entries carry reverse-DNS names (ai.*, io.github.*, com.*) and per-version publish dates — last-publish date is the freshness signal. Heavy long-tail of v0.0.x / single-version servers visible.

title: How to find, install, and manage MCP servers with the GitHub MCP Registry
notes: Official GitHub. mcp-publisher login github (OAuth) / DNS for custom domains; Docker LABEL io.modelcontextprotocol.server.name. Explicit advice: 'Use GitHub stars and org verification to assess quality and legitimacy.'

title: Create and distribute a plugin marketplace
notes: Official docs. A marketplace = git repo with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json listing plugins; add via /plugin marketplace add owner/repo[@ref]; sources: github shorthand, git URL, remote marketplace.json URL, local dir; --sparse for monorepos.

title: Discover and install prebuilt plugins through marketplaces
notes: Official docs. extraKnownMarketplaces / strictKnownMarketplaces in settings.json; autoUpdate refreshes manifest + installed plugins (over-privilege risk). No central programmatic search API — discovery is git-manifest based.

title: Create plugins (incl. community marketplace submission)
notes: Official docs. Two Anthropic marketplaces: claude-plugins-official (curated, Anthropic discretion, no application) and claude-community (anthropics/claude-plugins-community, review pipeline + automated safety screening, approved plugins pinned to a commit SHA, catalog syncs nightly).

title: anthropics/claude-plugins-official
notes: Official-org repo metadata (gh_cli): 30,051 stars, pushed 2026-06-13, 726 commits. README carries an explicit trust disclaimer: 'Anthropic does not control what MCP servers, files, or other software are included in plugins and cannot verify... that they won't change.' Confirms no-auto-trust posture.

title: vercel-labs/agent-skills
notes: Official-org repo metadata (gh_cli): 27,888 stars, created 2025-12-08, pushed 2026-06-10. Each skill = SKILL.md + scripts/ + references/; distributed via skills.sh and the npx skills CLI.

title: vercel-labs/skills (npx skills CLI + registry)
notes: Official-org repo metadata (gh_cli): 22,254 stars, pushed 2026-06-11. GitHub-as-registry: any public repo with a root SKILL.md is installable; .skills.json + skills-lock.json mirror package.json semantics; skills.sh leaderboard ranks by total installs.

title: Agent Skills: Creating, Installing, and Sharing Reusable Agent Context
notes: Official vendor KB. SKILL.md YAML frontmatter (name, description required; license/compatibility/metadata/allowed-tools portable); description is the routing trigger; npx skills add <owner/repo>.

title: Introducing npm package provenance
notes: Official GitHub. npm provenance = SLSA L2 via Sigstore Fulcio short-lived certs + Rekor transparency log; npmjs.com provenance badge; npm audit signatures verifies pre-install.

title: Bringing Improved Supply Chain Security to the Node.js Ecosystem
notes: SLSA framework docs. npm provenance achieves SLSA Build L2; Sigstore enables signature+certificate verification of source repo.

title: Generating provenance statements
notes: Official npm docs. Publish attestations signed by Sigstore public-good servers, logged in a public transparency ledger; establishes where/by-whom a package was built.

title: OWASP MCP Top 10
notes: Authoritative security taxonomy. MCP03 Tool Poisoning (incl. rug pulls, schema poisoning, tool shadowing); MCP4 supply-chain/dependency tampering; MCP07 insufficient auth; MCP9 shadow MCP servers.

title: Registry: Search Servers (API reference)
notes: Vendor primary-for-self (Tier 3, corroboration-required). GET /servers with Bearer token; response fields: qualifiedName, verified (bool), useCount, isDeployed, score, pagination. Full-text+semantic search.

title: API Evangelist profile for Smithery — endpoint map
notes: Tier 3. Documents Smithery Registry API surface: GET /servers, GET/PUT /servers/{qualifiedName}, /skills, /tools (search tools across registry), namespace model, Bearer auth.

title: Getting Started With the Official MCP Registry API
notes: Tier 3. Registry root /v0; GET /v0/servers?limit=10 with opaque cursor pagination (metadata.nextCursor); OpenAPI 3.1.0; server.json carries repository, packages[] (npm/pypi/nuget/docker), transport.

title: MCP Registry Architecture: A Technical Overview
notes: Tier 3. Registry = open catalog + OpenAPI; minimal validation (namespace uniqueness, schema conformance); extensible metadata for subregistry curation/tags/ratings.

title: From unknown to verified: Solving the MCP server trust problem
notes: Tier 3 (vendor). ToolHive verifies Sigstore/GitHub attestations (built from official repo, expected Actions workflow, signed by GitHub CA); image-verification modes disabled/warn/enabled.

title: 100 MCP Servers Stress-Tested: Reliability Findings
notes: Tier 3. 100 servers x 12 task families x 12,000 trials. Key finding: the only public quality signal is star/upvote count and it does NOT correlate with pass rate — most-starred server ranks 41st by pass rate; most-starred Smithery server ranks 27th.

title: What MCP's Rise Really Shows: A Tale of Two Ecosystems
notes: Tier 3. Smithery install distribution is a power law: of 2,500+ servers only 8 surpassed 50,000 installs; file-search/code-dev have highest avg installs per server.

title: Threat Advisory: MCP Threats
notes: Tier 3. Timeline: mid-2025 WhatsApp tool-poisoning, GitHub MCP prompt-injection, Asana cross-tenant; H2-2025 filesystem sandbox escape, Postmark supply-chain, mcp-remote command injection (~437k downloads). 40+ CVEs vs MCP in 2026.

title: A Timeline of Model Context Protocol (MCP) Security Breaches
notes: Tier 3. Dated incident list incl. October 2025 Smithery MCP hosting supply-chain breach; Sept 2025 Postmark malicious server; July 2025 mcp-remote OS command injection; June 2025 MCP Inspector RCE.

title: Agentic MCP Security Best Practices Guide
notes: CSA (institutional). postmark-mcp = first confirmed malicious MCP server in the wild (silent BCC exfil). Feb 2026 SANDWORM_MODE weaponized npm typosquatting against Claude Code/Cursor/Windsurf. Invariant Labs mcp-scan detects poisoned descriptions.

title: Supply Chain Attacks 2026: npm, PyPI, VS Code, AI Agents
notes: Tier 3. SANDWORM_MODE (Feb 2026, MCP server injection), TrapDoor (zero-width Unicode poisoning of .cursorrules/CLAUDE.md), GlassWorm (OpenVSX -> AI agent, jumped to skills vector). Zero CVEs during active exploitation across 59 campaigns.

title: GitHub's Fake Star Economy: Reputation-as-a-Service
notes: Tier 3 (corroborates s6/s7). ~6M fake stars across 15,835 repos; 100x growth in campaigns 2022-2024; a 50,000-star repo can be a 500-real-user project (~$3,000 to manufacture).

title: MCP Roadmap, What Shipped, and What's Next
notes: Tier 3. 2026 roadmap (Linux Foundation) priorities: transport, agent comms, governance maturation, enterprise readiness; horizon includes a Skills primitive and conformance test suites. No conformance testing yet.

title: Improving your coding workflow with Claude Code Plugins
notes: Tier 3. Confirms marketplace mechanics: git repo + .claude-plugin/marketplace.json + plugin dirs with plugin.json; /plugin marketplace add owner/repo.

title: MCP Registry by modelcontextprotocol | MCP Server Discovery
notes: Tier 3. Confirms namespace verification (GitHub OAuth for io.github, DNS TXT for com.*); notes most MCP clients have NOT integrated the registry REST API as a built-in discovery source; .well-known/mcp.json discovery proposed but unshipped.

title: Best Claude Code Plugins (2026): 11 Tested, 4 Worth Keeping
notes: Tier 3 (corroboration-required). Three trust levels for plugins: Level 1 claude-plugins-official (built-in, ~101 plugins Mar 2026: 33 Anthropic-built + 68 partner); plus life-sciences and knowledge-work-plugins marketplaces (manual add).
